tool_args.label: Americans with Disabilities Act Compliance Report December 2024

payload.title: Americans with Disabilities Act Compliance Report
payload.description: Standard Follow-Up Report: Annual update on ADA compliance at MTA New York City Transit, covering Key Station Plan, bus accessibility, oversight, outreach, training, platform edge detectable warning strips, signage, communication, maintenance of accessibility features, and paratransit.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: Annual ADA compliance report publisher and operator of subway and bus services.

payload.title: Senior Vice President, Stations Business Unit

payload.description: Parent authority. In June 2022, reached a class action settlement to add accessibility to 95 percent of currently inaccessible subway stations by 2055.
Governor who announced the MTA accessibility class action settlement in June 2022

payload.title: Chair and CEO
payload.description: Headed the MTA during the class action settlement on accessibility.
All-volunteer group of 18 community members established in February 2019 as successor to the CCC, committed to working with NYCT on accessibility issues. Held first meeting in June 2019.

Established as successor to the Transportation Disabled Committee, a forum for the disability community to provide input on Key Station selection and learn about accessibility initiatives. Succeeded by ACTA in February 2019.
Plan to make 100 subway stations accessible to people with disabilities by the year 2020, expanded from the original 54-station plan. Currently all 100 stations are accessible.
NYCT program started in 2018 committing to an additional 50 accessible stations, later increased to 70 stations in 2019. Goal: no subway rider more than two stops away from an accessible station.
Operates bus services referenced in ADA ramp/lift usage statistics.

payload.entity_name: Mayor's Office for People with Disabilities
payload.description: Receives annual reports on ADA compliance status within NYCT.
NYCT office that partnered with the Department of Subways in 2005 to administer the ADA Sensitivity Training Course.
NYCT department that partnered with the Office of ADA Compliance to administer the ADA Sensitivity Training Course in 2005.

tool_args.label: ADA CCC Meeting March 2000
tool_args.raw_text: Three proposals were presented for public comments at the ADA Compliance Coordination Committee (CCC) meeting on March 24, 2000.

payload.description: ADA Compliance Coordination Committee (CCC) meeting where three proposals for fine-tuning the Key Station Plan were presented for public comments.

tool_args.label: ACTA Established February 2019
tool_args.raw_text: In February 2019, the Advisory Committee for Transit Accessibility (ACTA) was established as a successor to the CCC.

payload.description: Advisory Committee for Transit Accessibility (ACTA) was established as a successor to the CCC.

tool_args.label: ACTA First Meeting June 2019
tool_args.raw_text: ACTA held its first meeting in June of 2019.

payload.description: ACTA held its first meeting.

tool_args.label: Public Event February 2019
tool_args.raw_text: A public event was held on February 6, 2019, where every geographic area across the system was reviewed and feedback was received on priority stations.

payload.description: Public event where every geographic area across the system was reviewed and feedback received on priority stations for accessibility.

tool_args.label: Class Action Settlement June 2022
tool_args.raw_text: In June 2022, Governor Kathy Hochul announced that the Metropolitan Transportation Authority and accessibility advocates reached a class action settlement...

payload.description: Governor Kathy Hochul announced the MTA and accessibility advocates reached a class action settlement affirming MTA's commitment to accessibility.

tool_args.label: ADA Training Course 2005
tool_args.raw_text: in 2005 the Office of ADA Compliance partnered with the Department of Subways to administer the ADA Sensitivity Training Course.

payload.description: Office of ADA Compliance partnered with the Department of Subways to administer the ADA Sensitivity Training Course.

tool_args.label: Wheelchair Ramp Users 2024
tool_args.raw_text: From January through December 2024, there were 1,330,758 customers who used the wheelchair ramp or lift to access MTA NYCT and MTA Bus compared to 1,256,659 in 2023.

payload.raw_value_text: 1,330,758 customers who used the wheelchair ramp or lift to access MTA NYCT and MTA Bus

tool_args.label: Wheelchair Ramp Users 2023
tool_args.raw_text: From January through December 2024, there were 1,330,758 customers who used the wheelchair ramp or lift to access MTA NYCT and MTA Bus compared to 1,256,659 in 2023. This was an increase of 5.90%.

tool_args.label: Wheelchair Ramp Usage Increase
tool_args.raw_text: This was an increase of 5.90%.

tool_args.label: DIS Equipped Buses
tool_args.raw_text: As of 12/31/2024, 4872 out of 5909, representing 82.4% of the fleet, are equipped with Digital Information Screens.

payload.raw_value_text: 4872 out of 5909, representing 82.4% of the fleet

tool_args.label: PA/CIS Units
tool_args.raw_text: All 472 NYCT subway stations have PA/CIS with more than 1,800 units installed throughout the system.

payload.raw_value_text: more than 1,800 units installed throughout the system
payload.description: Public Address Customer Information Screens (PA/CIS)

tool_args.label: Help Points Installed
tool_args.raw_text: More than 2,978 Help Point units were installed in all 472 NYCT subway stations.

payload.raw_value_text: More than 2,978 Help Point units were installed in all 472 NYCT subway stations

tool_args.label: Warning Strips Installed
tool_args.raw_text: At the end of 2024, a total of 432 stations have had ADA-specified platform edge warning strips installed.

payload.raw_value_text: 432 stations have had ADA-specified platform edge warning strips installed

tool_args.label: Station Inspections 2024
tool_args.raw_text: From January through December 2024, there were 14 accessible station inspections performed and over 871 deficiencies were found and referred for action.

tool_args.label: Deficiencies Found 2024
tool_args.raw_text: over 871 deficiencies were found and referred for action

payload.raw_value_text: over 871 deficiencies were found and referred for action

tool_args.label: Deficiencies Corrected 2024
tool_args.raw_text: In 2024 there were 736 deficiencies that were corrected, which includes deficiencies pending from prior years.

payload.raw_value_text: 736 deficiencies that were corrected, which includes deficiencies pending from prior years

tool_args.label: Bus Fleet Fully Accessible
tool_args.raw_text: The bus fleet, which has nearly 6,000 buses, is 100% accessible with wheelchair ramps or lifts, securement devices, public address systems, required priority seating signage, and kneeling features.

payload.claim_text: The bus fleet, which has nearly 6,000 buses, is 100% accessible with wheelchair ramps or lifts, securement devices, public address systems, required priority seating signage, and kneeling features.
payload.description: Full bus fleet wheelchair accessibility.

tool_args.label: Fast Forward Goal
tool_args.raw_text: The goal was to advance station accessibility to ensure that no subway rider was more than two stops away from an accessible station anywhere in the system.

payload.claim_text: The goal was to advance station accessibility to ensure that no subway rider was more than two stops away from an accessible station anywhere in the system.
payload.description: Fast Forward program goal for accessibility coverage.

tool_args.label: Settlement 95% Accessibility
tool_args.raw_text: Under the terms of the agreement the MTA, headed by Chair and CEO Janno Lieber, agreed to add accessibility to 95 percent of the currently inaccessible subway stations by having construction contracts in place by 2055.

payload.claim_text: MTA agreed to add accessibility to 95 percent of the currently inaccessible subway stations by having construction contracts in place by 2055.
payload.description: Class action settlement commitment.

tool_args.label: 56 Non-Key Stations Accessible
tool_args.raw_text: The following 56 Non-Key stations are wheelchair accessible, going beyond New York City Transit's Revised Key Station plan.

payload.claim_text: 56 Non-Key stations are wheelchair accessible, going beyond New York City Transit's Revised Key Station plan.
payload.description: Non-Key stations that are wheelchair accessible.

tool_args.label: Key Station Plan → CCC Meeting
tool_args.raw_text: Three proposals were presented for public comments at the ADA Compliance Coordination Committee (CCC) meeting on March 24, 2000. The first proposal was to add 2 stations... to the Key Station Plan.

payload.description: The CCC meeting presented proposals for fine-tuning the Key Station Plan.

tool_args.label: Fast Forward → Public Event
tool_args.raw_text: In 2019, NYCT consulted extensively with the disability community... to select stations to be prioritized for accessibility. A public event was held on February 6, 2019.

payload.description: Public event held to select stations to be prioritized for accessibility under Fast Forward.

tool_args.label: Fast Forward → Settlement
tool_args.raw_text: Furthermore in 2018, NYCT committed to an additional 50 accessible stations as part of a program called Fast Forward... In June 2022... a class action settlement that affirms the MTA's commitment towards accessibility in the subway system.

payload.description: The class action settlement affirmed MTA's commitment towards accessibility, following Fast Forward.

tool_args.label: Revised Key Station Plan → NYCT
tool_args.raw_text: The following reports on our progress in complying with the Revised Key Station Plan

payload.description: MTA New York City Transit implements the Revised Key Station Plan.

tool_args.label: Fast Forward → NYCT
tool_args.raw_text: in 2018, NYCT committed to an additional 50 accessible stations as part of a program called Fast Forward.

payload.description: NYCT committed to the Fast Forward program for additional accessible stations.

tool_args.label: ACTA successor of CCC
tool_args.raw_text: In February 2019, the Advisory Committee for Transit Accessibility (ACTA) was established as a successor to the CCC.

payload.description: ACTA was established as a successor to the CCC in February 2019.

tool_args.label: MTA → Wheelchair Ramp 2024
tool_args.raw_text: there were 1,330,758 customers who used the wheelchair ramp or lift to access MTA NYCT and MTA Bus

payload.description: MTA wheelchair ramp/lift usage metric for 2024.

tool_args.label: MTA → Ramp Usage Increase
tool_args.raw_text: This was an increase of 5.90%.

payload.description: MTA wheelchair ramp usage increase metric.

tool_args.label: NYCT → Station Inspections
tool_args.raw_text: From January through December 2024, there were 14 accessible station inspections performed

payload.description: NYCT accessible station inspections metric for 2024.

tool_args.label: NYCT → Bus Fleet Accessible
tool_args.raw_text: The bus fleet... is 100% accessible with wheelchair ramps or lifts...

payload.description: NYCT makes the claim that the bus fleet is 100% accessible.
